title: Count Square Submatrices with All Ones|(DP-56)

content.problemStatement:
Given an n * m matrix of ones and zeros, return how many square submatrices have
    all ones.
content.inputFormat: The input consists of three parameters: n (number of rows), m (number of columns), and a 2D array arr of size n x m containing 0s and 1s.
content.outputFormat: The output is the total count of square submatrices with all ones.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use dynamic programming to build a table where each cell represents the size of the largest square submatrix ending at that cell.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    # Function to count total square submatrices with all 1s
    def countSquares(self, n, m, arr):
        # DP table to store size of largest square ending at (i,j)
        dp = [[0]*m for _ in range(n)]

        # Initialize first row
        for j in range(m):
            dp[0][j] = arr[0][j]

        # Initialize first column
        for i in range(n):
            dp[i][0] = arr[i][0]

        # Fill the rest of DP table
        for i in range(1, n):
            for j in range(1, m):
                # If current cell is 0, no square ends here
                if arr[i][j] == 0:
                    dp[i][j] = 0
                else:
                    # Minimum of top, left, top-left + 1
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i-1][j-1], dp[i][j-1])

        # Sum up all values in DP table
        total = sum(sum(row) for row in dp)

        # Return total count of squares
        return total

# Input matrix
arr = [
    [0, 1, 1, 1],
    [1, 1, 1, 1],
    [0, 1, 1, 1]
]

# Matrix dimensions
n, m = 3, 4

# Create Solution object
obj = Solution()

# Call function
squares = obj.countSquares(n, m, arr)

# Print the result
print("The number of squares:", squares)
